title: Running blind with ABCD in the datacard

posts.name: Peter Major
posts.text:
*** Discussion title: Higgs Combination
Dear Experts,
I have implemented the ABCD method in our datacard as described in
https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part2/settinguptheanalysis/#rate-parameters
An example of my implementation is available here:
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1H_iemGQqnLKYJ5kZrBbnaEHl-AF1VX4H?usp=sharing
I am wondering if it is possible to run blind with this datacard
somehow. Unfortunately ```combine -M AsymptoticLimits -d
./combinedCard.csv --run blind``` leads to a lot of error messages,
which only makes sense, as the ABCD implementation is basically ruined
by this option.
The error messages are also saved and are accessible on the link above.
Thank you for your input!
posts.cooked_html:
*** Discussion title: Higgs Combination

Dear Experts,

I have implemented the ABCD method in our datacard as described in
https://cms-analysis.github.io/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/part2/settinguptheanalysis/#rate-parameters

An example of my implementation is available here:
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1H_iemGQqnLKYJ5kZrBbnaEHl-AF1VX4H?usp=sharing

I am wondering if it is possible to run blind with this datacard
somehow. Unfortunately ```combine -M AsymptoticLimits -d
./combinedCard.csv --run blind``` leads to a lot of error messages,
which only makes sense, as the ABCD implementation is basically ruined
by this option.

The error messages are also saved and are accessible on the link above.

Thank you for your input!
